title: EARLY ASSESSMENT AND SUPPORT ALLIANCE - 211info
text: LIFEWORKS NW BEAVERTON EARLY ASSESSMENT AND SUPPORT ALLIANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The LifeWorks NW Early Assessment and Support Alliance (EASA) identifies youth and young adults with symptoms of psychosis as early as possible to provide effective support and treatment. Services include medication and medication monitoring, individual and group counseling, multifamily groups, occupational therapy, family education and support, vocational training, life skills coaching, 24/7 crisis services, links to community resources, and education about psychosis. EASA also works closely with family members and others who are supportive of the individual to help them succeed. 10700 SW Beaverton-Hillside Highway Suite 110 Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: Accepts OHP, private insurances, and no insurance. No fees to participants regardless of insurance status. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-6pm Email (503) 645-9010 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 645-9010 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call referral line for intake, email for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Insurance information, if applicable. LANGUAGES: English, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Washington County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: TriMet bus 48 on Cornell Road Services: Family Counseling Psychiatric Case Management Other Services Offered At This Location: INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - PROMOTORA MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - OUTPATIENT MENTAL HEALTH CARE MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - PARENT-CHILD INTERACTIVE THERAPY MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - SEVERE MENTAL ILLNESS SERVICES MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - TRANSITION-AGE YOUTH SERVICES SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - ADULT SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER TREATMENT SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - WOMEN'S RESIDENTIAL SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - YOUTH SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER TREATMENT If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
